Prompts.Lab 1: Generate a Rule Registry Manifest for the following set of Rubrics. Verify (3 + 13) + (3 + 4 + 2 + 4 + 3 + 2) + (14 + 9 + 13) = 70 Rules, grouped into 11 Rubrics.
Prompts.Lab 2: Generate a Rule Registry Manifest for the following set of Rubrics. Verify (3 + 13) + (3 + 2 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 2) + (14 + 9 + 13) = 69 Rules, grouped into 12 Rubrics.
Prompts.Lab 3: Generate a Rule Registry Manifest for the following set of Rubrics. (3 + 13) + (5 + 6 + 3 + 6 + 7 + 6 + 7 + 4) + (14 + 9 + 13) = 96 Rules, grouped into 13 Rubrics.
Prompts.Lab 4: Generate a Rule Registry Manifest for the following set of Rubrics. Verify (3 + 13) + (4 + 3 + 4 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 1 + 3) + (14 + 9 + 13) = 82 Rules, grouped into 15 Rubrics.
Prompts.Lab 5: Generate a Rule Registry Manifest for the following set of Rubrics. Verify (3 + 13) + (3 + 10 + 7 + 10 + 5) + (14 + 9 + 13) = 87 Rules, grouped into 10 Rubrics.
Prompts.Lab 6: Generate a Rule Registry Manifest for the following set of Rubrics. Verify (3 + 13) + (7 + 6 + 5 + 10) + (14 + 9 + 13) = 80 Rules, grouped into 9 Rubrics.
Prompts.Lab 7: Generate a Rule Registry Manifest for the following set of Rubrics.
Prompts.Assess: Act as a deterministic grading agent. Perform an Exhaustive Row-by-Row Audit for this report. Evaluate the report against every criterion listed in the Rubrics and display your conclusions about each and every Rule. For each individual Rule: 1) State the Rule ID 2) State the Rule Name and Description 3) Quote the specific text or data from the report that satisfies or fails the Rule. 4) Assign a binary score (1 or 0) based on that evidence. Constraints: Do not use synthesized headers; only use the Rubric Titles found in the PDF. Strictly follow the list order; present every bold bullet item as a standalone Rule in the exact sequence they appear in the document. Do not skip any Rules; if an item is missing, explicitly state "Criteria not found in report" and award 0 points for that Rule. Acknowledge only PDF text: do not categorize, summarize, or describe the Rules using any other language. Do not provide conversational feedback; only output the breakdown for each Rule. Output the results in a csv formatted table, having the following column names in the first row: "Rule ID", "Score", "Rule Name", and "Evidence"
